prompt:
I just merged the program-printing branch into the lane roster module and now the whole thing is broken, in two acts.

Act one: it won't even import. Python throws a TabError somewhere in the printing code, at a line that looks EXACTLY like its neighbours in my editor — I've diffed it by eye five times and I can't tell what it wants. We standardise on 4-space indents, for the record.

Act two: once I hand-mangled enough whitespace locally to get past that (don't ship what I did, I reverted it), the roster itself came out nonsense at our test meet: every swimmer who actually has a seed time vanished from the heats, and at meets with no exhibition heats the untimed kids showed up IN the seeded heats and then the whole program blew up trying to sort them. The intended rules are in the module docstring and they haven't changed: timed swimmers race seeded, untimed swim exhibition only when the meet allows it, otherwise they sit out.

So something in that merge went wrong in more than one place. Clean it up properly — python3 test_laneroster.py is the bar, and the test file stays as-is. Don't rewrite the module, it was fine before the merge; just fix what the merge broke.
